description_long: This is a small, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic low-wing configuration. The body is predominantly white with red accents on the nose, wingtips, and tail, and features black and gray striping along the sides. The registration number 'F-IAOA' is clearly marked on the wings and fuselage, and the number '18' is displayed on the tail. The cockpit is enclosed with clear windows, positioned at the front of the fuselage, and appears to be designed for one or two occupants. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a smaller nose wheel. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the tail features a conventional single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
description: A small, white low-wing airplane with red accents, a single nose-mounted propeller, and fixed tricycle landing gear. It features an enclosed cockpit and prominent registration markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, red nose, cockpit windows, and the 'F-IAOA' registration on the wing and fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose-mounted propeller, cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical low-wing layout with red-tipped wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the '18' marking on the tail, the propeller, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the trailing edges of the wings with red tips.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and slight dihedral; no visible winglets or variable geometry.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
